zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Blake於1626年9月3日出生在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特時，他的父親William Blake11623歲，母親Agnes Thorne33歲。他於1653年與Patience Pope在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1692年9月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可米爾頓享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Blake于1626年9月3日出生在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特时，他的父亲William Blake11623岁，母亲Agnes Thorne33岁。他于1653年与Patience Pope在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1692年9月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可米爾頓享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (England and central Scotland): variant of Black 1, meaning ‘swarthy’ or ‘dark-haired’, from a byform of the Old English adjective blæc, blac ‘black’, with change of vowel length.

English: nickname from Middle English blak(e) (Old English blāc) ‘wan, pale, white, fair’. In Middle English the two words blac and blāc, with opposite meanings, fell together as Middle English blake. In the absence of independent evidence as to whether the person referred to was dark or fair, it is now impossible to tell which sense was originally meant.
English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English bleik, blaik>, blek(e) (Old Norse bleikr) ‘pale or sallow’ (in complexion).

variants.name: Bleak
meanings: English: mostly a variant of   Blake  , but perhaps also of   Blick  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blaikie
meanings: Scottish: nickname from a pet form of   Blake  .

variants.name: Blakes
meanings: English: variant of Blake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Eudard
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Eduard
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Blake was born on 3 September 1626, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, his father, William Blake, was 11623 and his mother, Agnes Thorne, was 33. He married Patience Pope in 1653, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 3 September 1692, in Milton, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 66.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Blake è nato il il 3 settembre 1626 nel Pitminster, Somerset, England, il suo padre, William Blake, aveva 11623 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Thorne, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Patience Pope nel 1653, nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 3 settembre 1692, nel Milton, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Blakeが1626年9月3日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで生まれた時，父William Blakeは11623歳，母Agnes Thorneは33歳であった。1653年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンでPatience Popeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1692年9月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクミルトンで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Blake(이)가 1626년 9월 3일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Blake(은)는 11623세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Thorne(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1653년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 Patience Pope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1692년 9월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 미턴에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Blake родился 3 сентября 1626 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени William Blake было 11623 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Thorne, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Patience Pope в 1653 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он умер 3 сентября 1692 г. в местности милтон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Blake nació el 3 de septiembre de 1626, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, William Blake, tenía 11623 años y su mamá, Agnes Thorne, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Patience Pope en 1653, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 3 de septiembre de 1692, en Milton, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 66 años.
